title: stylidium exiguum
intro: Stylidium exiguum, also known as the small triggerplant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Stylidiaceae. This plant is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Stylidium exiguum include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
